title: Understand: The Civil War and Emancipation
description: Check your understanding of the Civil War and Emancipation with this set of multiple-choice questions.
topic_path: America’s Second Founding: Slavery, the Civil War, Emancipation, and Reconstruction
Understand: The Civil War and Emancipation

assessment_items.question_content:
**Drawing on his war powers as president, Abraham Lincoln issued which historic declaration about slavery on January 1, 1863?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The Declaration of Sentiments
The Gettysburg Address
The Emancipation Proclamation
The Civil Rights Act

assessment_items.question_content:
**What was the effect of the Kansas-Nebraska Act?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: An outbreak of violence in Kansas
The addition of Kansas as a free state
Kansas remained a territory
The ruling in *Dred Scott v. Sandford*

assessment_items.question_content:
**What was one effect of the election of 1860?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The secession of the southern states
The formation of the Republican party
The passage of the Kansas-Nebraska Act
The presidency of James Buchanan

assessment_items.question_content:
**What action did Lincoln take at the beginning of the Civil War that critics said violated civil liberties? **

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Suspended the writ of habeas corpus
Declared war against the southern states
Banned the institution of slavery
Expelled slaveholding states from the Union

assessment_items.question_content:
**What is one reason that Lincoln did not ban slavery outright at the beginning of the Civil War?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: He believed that, as president, he did not have the power to ban slavery everywhere in America.
He was a supporter of the institution of slavery and did not want to abolish it.
Congress had already passed a law banning slavery, but the Confederate states had ignored it.
He wanted the Supreme Court to abolish slavery on constitutional grounds to make the ban more secure.

assessment_items.question_content:
**What was the goal of the Emancipation Proclamation?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: To free all enslaved people living in territories then in rebellion against the United States
To abolish slavery everywhere in the United States, including in the Confederate states
To free all enslaved people living in the border states of Kentucky, Delaware, Maryland, and Missouri
To allow all citizens to choose whether their state should be a free state or a slaveholding state

assessment_items.question_content:
**What part of the Constitution did Lincoln use to support his decision to issue the Emancipation Proclamation?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Article II, Section 2
Article III, Section 1
The Ninth Amendment
The Tenth Amendment

assessment_items.question_content:
**What TWO founding documents did Lincoln refer to, directly or indirectly, in his Gettysburg Address?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The Declaration of Independence
The U.S. Constitution
The Articles of Confederation
The Declaration of Sentiments

assessment_items.question_content:
**How did the border states of Missouri, Kentucky, Maryland, and Delaware complicate the issue of slavery?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Lincoln wanted to find a constitutional way to ban slavery, but he did not want the border states to secede.
Lincoln wanted to entice the border states back to the Union, as they had seceded over the issue of slavery.
Lincoln wanted to encourage the spread of slavery in the border states, as enslaved labor was important in raising funds for the war effort.
Lincoln did not want to issue any laws regarding slavery until the border states could form representative governments of their own.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Why was slavery considered to be a state issue?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: There was no federal slave code.
There was no federal legislation legalizing slavery.
There was a constitutional amendment that banned slavery at the federal level.
There were no state laws that banned slavery.

assessment_items.question_content:
**What was Lincoln’s primary goal at the start of the Civil War?**


[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: To abolish the institution of slavery across North America and immediately free all enslaved Black Americans
To stop the Confederate states from annexing the border states of Delaware, Maryland, Kentucky, Missouri, and West Virginia
To found a new nation with no history of slavery and based on the principles of equality and the rule of law
To preserve the Union by reclaiming the states that had seceded and formed the Confederacy

assessment_items.question_content:
**What was one of the goals of the Gettysburg Address?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: To free all enslaved people in the Confederacy
To increase the president’s wartime powers
To convince the Confederacy to surrender and rejoin the Union
To redefine the Civil War as a fight for freedom and equality
